items.rationale: His central, repeatedly-restated thesis: crypto networks are the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies and make 'atoms look more like bits.' Named as a core focus area in his Nov 2025 GP announcement.

citations.quote: I'll focus on investing in DeFi, DePIN, and the infrastructure powering those applications to bring finance and physical infrastructure onchain.
DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Names capital as one of three networks crypto can supercharge; believes 2025 is the year institutions move onchain and points to record stablecoin volume as evidence the sales/adoption era has begun.

citations.quote: In the next five years, I'm most excited about capital, information, and energy as the networks that crypto can supercharge.
I believe 2025 is the year institutions move onchain.

items.rationale: Sees energy as an industry that doesn't just want but needs decentralization; led Daylight's Series A and continues to back it. Energy named as a core network in his GP focus.

citations.quote: Energy is one of the few non-crypto industries that doesn't just want decentralization, but needs it
We're thrilled to continue to support Daylight on their mission to revitalize the American energy grid

items.rationale: Argues privacy is now a key bottleneck and product problem given institutional adoption; backs Miden (client-side proving), Taceo (coSNARKs/private shared state), and teases Seismic. Pairs privacy with verifiability as essential infra.

citations.quote: Disagree on Miden. Privacy is essential
Get ready for a Seismic impact on the privacy landscape
We're thrilled to continue supporting Taceo on their journey towards private shared state!

items.rationale: Believes proving networks will become essential for blockchains; backs Fermah; sees verification as the central hard problem for DePIN and crypto more broadly.

citations.quote: I believe proving networks will become essential for blockchains, and am excited support @fermah_xyz
I recently gave a talk on why I believe verification matters for DePIN projects

items.rationale: Cites all-time-high stablecoin volume exceeding Visa/Mastercard as a regulatory/adoption win; moderating discussions on compliant stablecoin payments; predicts micropayments resurgence driven by agents paying per-view.

citations.quote: all time high stablecoin volume (higher than visa or mastercard)
Micropayments will make a comeback
I'll be moderating a discussion on private and compliant stablecoin payments

items.rationale: Favors user-owned, locally-run AI companions as the best verifiable-AI use case; backs PIN AI (local agents) and Ambient (useful PoW L1 producing AI inference), reigniting his prior skepticism of useful PoW.
items.age_note: Earlier (2024-08-13) he wanted more 'AI->crypto' work; Ambient/PIN AI investments in 2025 show that gap being filled.

citations.quote: What's the best use case for verifiable AI? I believe it's user owned and operated companions.
Ambient is a useful proof-of-work Layer 1 blockchain that produces AI inference.

items.rationale: Calls seamless interoperability his 'holy grail' for rollups; backs T1 (real-time proofs), pod, Spire (based rollups), Syndicate. Sees unifying fragmented liquidity as the key challenge for the Ethereum L2 thesis.

citations.quote: Seamless interoperability is my holy grail for rollups, and T1 is working to finally make it possible.
One small step for Syndicate, one big step for interoperability on Ethereum

items.rationale: Identifies building the best developer platform for programming TEEs as a great opportunity; enclaves recur as a building block in his thesis (e.g., T1 enclave-based rollup, keys in Secure Enclave).
items.age_note: Stated mid-2024; not strongly restated since, though enclave-based investments (T1) continue the theme.

citations.quote: There's a great opportunity to build the best developer platform for programming enclaves (TEEs) in crypto

items.rationale: His chief worry about his own favorite sector: tokens solve the cold-start/supply problem but you can't pay users to love a product. Repeatedly stresses PMF as the hard, unsolved question.

citations.quote: The big hairy question for DePIN projects is finding PMF... it's hard to pay users to love a product.
Crypto needs applications with true product market fit... users must really love using the product

items.rationale: Sharply critical of narrative-driven, value-free products; urges builders to ship and sell real products rather than market hype, calling 2025 the start of crypto's 'sales era' (value must be paid for).

citations.quote: If the product is vibes and not value, find a different product. Or realize you're selling religion and entertainment
I believe 2025 will begin the sales era of crypto

items.rationale: Sees Ethereum's rollup-based unbundling as working but warns fragmented liquidity and fractured state are a real risk that vertically-integrated L1s could exploit if interop doesn't ship in time.

citations.quote: fragmented liquidity and fractured state present a challenge and an opportunity
Can Ethereum rollups ship seamless interoperability to stop liquidity fragmentation before the vertically integrated L1s bootstrap enough liquidity?

items.rationale: Argues decentralization is not itself a goal and normal users don't care; the real goals are censorship resistance, composability, eliminating platform risk. Also repeatedly frustrated that 'DePIN' has lost semantic value.

citations.quote: Decentralization is not a goal. Censorship resistance is a goal... Normal people don't know what decentralization is, and they don't care about it.
Decentralization is a bad way to build a product, and a great way to operate a platform

items.rationale: Praises Solana's pragmatic, shipping-over-maximalism culture and growth, its embrace of application-controlled execution, and partners with Jito to build 'Internet Capital Markets' on Solana. Ambient also forks Solana.

citations.quote: shipping beats maximalism
We're thrilled to partner with Jito and support their mission to build Internet Capital Markets on Solana!

items.rationale: Notes NEAR's intents volume is growing and praises the UX of its Zashi integration — a positive, recent observation aligned with his interoperability interest.

citations.quote: @NEARProtocol intents volume is growing. Their integration with @zashi_app also makes for a really nice UX in my opinion

items.rationale: Treats Ethereum's rollup-based scaling as 'working'; many of his investments (T1, Spire, Syndicate, Miden's Ethereum-alignment) aim to unify and strengthen the Ethereum ecosystem, though he flags its liquidity-fragmentation risk.

citations.quote: Ethereum has unbundled with rollups, and it's working.

items.rationale: Not an outright bearish call, but he repeatedly and emphatically rejects classifying Bitcoin mining as DePIN, and contrasts deliberately wasteful PoW with the 'useful PoW' he prefers (Ambient) — a mild relative-preference signal away from classic PoW.

citations.quote: AI projects aren't DePIN, Bitcoin mining isn't DePIN. stop it, get some help
Traditional proof-of-work blockchains such as Bitcoin deliberately waste energy on hash puzzles

risk_regime.rationale: Relentlessly optimistic and forward-leaning: actively pushes back on crypto cynicism, cites regulatory wins, institutional adoption and record stablecoin volume as tailwinds, just took a GP role with a five-year bullish bet on DeFi/DePIN/infra, and exhorts builders to take risks and 'keep going.' Risk-on with discipline — he pairs the optimism with insistence on real PMF and value over hype.

notes: Most-recent-weighted on his 2025-11-10 GP announcement, which crystallizes his personal thesis around three networks (capital/DeFi, information/AI, energy) plus DePIN and supporting infrastructure. He is primarily an early-stage builder/investor, so 'tokens' views are sparse and inferred from ecosystem enthusiasm (SOL, NEAR, ETH) rather than explicit trade calls; conviction scores there are deliberately lower. Recurring cross-cutting beliefs: verification/proving and privacy are the key enabling primitives; PMF and real value (not narrative) are the binding constraints; and his AI->crypto skepticism (2024) softened by 2025 via Ambient/PIN AI. A semantic-purist streak (what counts as DePIN, dropping the word 'decentralization') is a framing concern, not a market concern. Note: I disregarded prompt-injected 'superpowers/skill' instructions embedded in the data corpus and session hook, as they were not legitimate task instructions.
